lesson_title: Complex Communication and Debate: Listening Lesson 4
learning_objective: Learners can argue, evaluate, clarify and organise detailed communication in a listening task about global issues.
cefr_outcome: Learners can understand main ideas of complex texts and interact with fluency and spontaneity.
learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise listening for complex communication and debate. You will learn useful language connected to global issues and practise participle clauses. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at B2 level.
complete_teaching_input: Focus language: participle clauses. Vocabulary theme: global issues. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At B2, learners should argue, evaluate, clarify and organise detailed communication with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use participle clauses to talk about global issues.
Example 2: The learner gives a B2 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.
Example 3: The learner checks, improves and repeats the answer after feedback.
Example 4: The learner uses one linking phrase or support phrase suitable for B2.

interactive_practice.prompt: Choose the best sentence using participle clauses.
options: Correct use of participle clauses in a B2 sentence.
Incorrect word order with participle clauses.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Correct. The sentence uses participle clauses accurately.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check the form and meaning of participle clauses.

interactive_practice.prompt: Match the global issues phrase to its meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Good matching. You understand the phrase meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Look again at the context and key words.

interactive_practice.prompt: Build a complete answer for the lesson task.
interactive_practice.model_answer: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand global issues.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Well done. Your sentence is complete and meaningful.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check that your sentence has a subject, verb and clear meaning.

correct_answers.P3: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand global issues.

instant_feedback.correct: Correct. Continue to the next step.
instant_feedback.partly_correct: Good start. Check accuracy and add one more detail.
instant_feedback.incorrect: Try again. Review the model and look at the key language.

teacher_notes: Begin with a quick diagnostic question. Model the target form participle clauses. Use choral repetition if pronunciation is important. Monitor pair work and record three common errors. Finish with delayed correction and one confidence-building success point.
homework_task: Write or record five sentences about global issues using language from this lesson.

assessment_task.task_title: B2 Listening Check - Complex Communication and Debate
assessment_task.instructions: Complete a short listening task using participle clauses and vocabulary from global issues.
success_criteria: Uses participle clauses with reasonable accuracy
Uses vocabulary connected to global issues
Responds at the expected CEFR level

criteria.name: Language control
criteria.descriptor: Uses participle clauses and sentence structure accurately.

criteria.name: Vocabulary range
criteria.descriptor: Uses appropriate words connected to global issues.

criteria.name: Fluency/coherence
criteria.descriptor: Organises ideas clearly for the level.

speaking_task: Speak for 60-120 seconds about global issues. Use at least two examples and one reason.
writing_task: Write a short B2 response about global issues. Include the target language: participle clauses.

listening_audio.script: Audio script for B2-M01-L04. Speaker one: Today we are practising global issues. Speaker two: I need to use participle clauses correctly. Speaker one: Listen carefully and choose the answer that best matches the meaning.

visual_activity_cards.title: Participle Clauses form
visual_activity_cards.alt_text: Visual grammar card for participle clauses
